mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS signals strengthening household demand and improving near-term revenue expectations for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month average marks active monetary tightening that raises discount rates and lowers equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening observations have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform non-triggered observations by at least 50 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS also rises, markets price both margin pressure and tighter policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS rise state fails to predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either condition alone, or if interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise trigger instances do not have lower average 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered instances, or if negative-hit rate is below 55% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 6-month growth in INDPRO signals expanding real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward sorts, or if the top-minus-bottom tercile spread is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and future pressure on construction, durable goods, and credit-sensitive earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform baseline returns by at least 75 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while RSAFS growth turns negative, firms face labor-cost stickiness against weakening sales, compressing expected margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth crosses below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive and RSAFS-negative interaction does not predict lower 20-day SPX returns than PAYEMS or RSAFS single-series signals, or if the interaction term is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher GDPC1 growth confirms broad real demand expansion and lowers near-term earnings recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after the signal is observed.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth crosses above zero
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth-crossing observations fail to deliver higher 5-day SPX returns than non-crossing observations, or if event-window mean return is not positive out of sample.
